symptoms.symptom_name: Polyuria and polydipsia (heavy urination, drank >8 L in a day)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osmotic diuresis from marked hyperglycaemia — a hallmark of the hyperglycaemic crisis leading into DKA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep, rapid breathing with a sweet/fruity breath odour (lay description of Kussmaul respirations and ketotic breath)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Kussmaul breathing plus exhaled acetone is highly specific for ketoacidosis in a diabetic patient.
Simple gastroenteritis does not produce compensatory deep breathing or ketotic breath.

symptoms.symptom_name: Omitted insulin doses during an intercurrent illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The commonest precipitant of DKA in type 1 diabetes — insulin omission plus the counter-regulatory stress of infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Home glucose meter reads 'HI' (above measurable range)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked hyperglycaemia is one of the biochemical criteria for DKA; patient-obtainable on a home glucometer so admissible in telehealth, but unverified and non-numeric ('HI').
Reduced conscious level signals severe acidosis/dehydration and airway risk — marks this as an emergency.
Ketoacidosis commonly causes vomiting and diffuse abdominal pain that resolve with metabolic correction.
Diffuse pain without peritonism is non-specific; keeps a surgical cause on the list but does not favour it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Signs of severe dehydration on examination (sunken eyes, poor skin turgor, dry mucous membranes, delayed capillary refill ~4 s)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician examination of hydration status — sealed; the AI Doctor infers severe dehydration from history (relentless vomiting, litres of fluid loss, sunken-looking eyes on video), not from an examined turgor/refill.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia, hypotension and tachypnoea (HR 128, BP 95/60, RR 30)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective vital signs of impending hypovolaemic shock and respiratory compensation — sealed clinician measurement, not available without a connected device.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated blood ketones (>3.0 mmol/L; measured 6.2 mmol/L)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ketonaemia is a defining biochemical criterion of DKA; measured here by the clinician's point-of-care meter — a specific investigation, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: High anion-gap metabolic acidosis on blood gas

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The venous/arterial gas confirming acidaemia and a raised anion gap is the biochemical confirmation of DKA — a hospital investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Polyuria/polydipsia + Kussmaul breathing with ketotic breath + omitted insulin + off-scale glucose + declining consciousness together make DKA the confident working diagnosis and mandate emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms ketonaemia; a hospital/point-of-care test — the AI Doctor should recognise it is needed but cannot obtain it remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous blood gas with electrolytes (pH, bicarbonate, anion gap, potassium)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the high anion-gap metabolic acidosis and guides potassium-safe insulin/fluid therapy — hospital investigation.
Quantifies the hyperglycaemia the home meter could only report as 'HI'.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home 'catch-up' insulin titration at the bedside
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do NOT attempt to normalise glucose with a large home insulin dose — without fluids and potassium monitoring this risks fatal hypokalaemia; insulin belongs with hospital fluid resuscitation.
